features.description: Governance core, RG001/RG005, dispatcher, hooks, and controlled updates
RG002/RG003/RG004 and five Codex Skills
GitHub enforcement, reusable workflow, release, and supply-chain verification
Preset-driven bootstrap, adoption checks, hook connection, and stable automation reports
Explicit new and clone automation with safe rollback and offline E2E coverage
Deterministic prepare-pr reporting and clearer first-push diagnostics
Agent-neutral governance playbooks and thin Codex Skill adapters
Claude Code adapter and cross-agent contract consistency
Read-only preflight classification, stable report contract, locked-engine inspection, and strict hook diagnosis
Shared Agent gate Playbook, Codex Skill adapter, and optional explicitly enabled Codex hook templates
Claude Code gate command, optional hook templates, and cross-adapter decision contract consistency
User-level Agent authorization policy with deterministic path matching and explicit preset constraints
Bilingual documentation, packaging, version 1.1.1 release metadata, and end-to-end acceptance
Version-aware launcher routing, PATH command entry, install/update migration, and CLI output contracts
User-level engine registry, repository registration, engine listing, and safe pruning
Verified release catalog, explicit version check, cached upgrade advisory, and read-only preflight reporting
Version 1 execution contract feature ledger and implementation boundaries
Versioned engine manifests, stable dispatcher protocol checks, and downgrade prevention
RG006 runtime registry, dependency preparation definition hashes, and ordered execution graphs
Workflow consumer, revision source, checkout, cache, action, and execution-context verification
Remote-aware canonical base resolution for pre-push and CI event revisions
Isolated execution verification for CI and candidate commits with clean-checkout evidence
Wrapper and sidecar preservation with per-tip isolated pre-push verification
Preset migration, required checks, release metadata, bilingual documentation, and end-to-end acceptance
